title: rhizophoraceae
intro: The Rhizophoraceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants. This family, commonly known as the mangrove family, comprises about 120 species of trees and shrubs that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical coastal regions. Rhizophoraceae plants are known for their unique ability to thrive in salty, waterlogged environments and are often characterized by their distinctive, woody growth habits and extensive root systems. The family Rhizophoraceae is a key component of mangrove ecosystems, providing essential habitat and food sources for a wide variety of marine life.
